label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. Jane booked a flight for tomorrow, which is July 29, 2002. Wait, hold on. If today is the day she's booking it, and she's booking for tomorrow, then tomorrow would be July 29th? That would mean today is July 28th, 2002. But the question is about what the date is 24 hours later. So the flight is on July 29th. Adding 24 hours to that date. But wait, adding 24 hours to a date usually brings you to the same time the next day. For example, if it's 12 PM on July 29th, adding 24 hours would be 12 PM on July 30th. So regardless of the time of day, adding 24 hours should move the date by exactly one day. So if the flight is on July 29th, 24 hours later would be July 30th, 2002. Let me check the options. Looking at the options, option C is 07/30/2002. That seems correct. But let me make sure there's no trick here. Is 2002 a leap year? Well, 2002 isn't a leap year. Leap years are divisible by 4, but 2002 isn't divisible by 4. So February had 28 days. But July has 31 days. So July 29th plus one day is the 30th, then the 31st, then
